summary: Frontend-only regression validation passed for continuous-page editor layout on public URL. Verified single continuous canvas rendering, inline section dividers, in-page section navigation, comments rail open/interaction, history rail open, and no load-time regressions.
action_items: No blocking fixes required for requested scope.
Optional refactor: split /app/frontend/src/components/editor/EditorWorkspace.jsx (1577 lines) into smaller modules/hooks for maintainability and safer regression surface.
critical_code_review_comments: Continuous page implementation is correctly centralized via one canvas node (data-testid=editor-paper-page-continuous) and inline section markers derived from sectionStartIds/pageMap.
handlePageChange -> focusBlock uses block refs with scrollIntoView, keeping section jumps in the same document canvas instead of swapping canvases.
EditorWorkspace.jsx exceeds 700 lines; per maintainability guideline this is a risk area for future regressions and should be modularized.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated against https://realtime-editor.stage-preview.emergentagent.com. Playwright run confirms: single continuous page canvas count=1, section dividers for page-1/page-2/page-3 visible, page nav buttons remain functional, comments and history rails still open. Screenshots captured: /app/test_reports/iteration5_editor_loaded.jpg and /app/test_reports/iteration5_post_checks.jpg. Inline AI suggestions and collaboration preview are MOCKED frontend behaviors.
rca of the issue: No blocking issue reproduced in this scope. Continuous layout works because rendering now maps all blocks in one paper container and uses inline section dividers keyed to first block per section rather than separate page canvases.
